strengths: 19+ years of hands-on software engineering across web, mobile, embedded, and high-throughput backend systems (e.g., low-latency services handling millions of requests/sec at Dunnhumby)
Entrepreneurial track record as founder of GISCollective, an open-source geo-data platform deployed on Kubernetes and used by Green Map
Recent AI-agent infrastructure exposure at re:cinq, including MCP code generators and frameworks for agentic workflows
concerns: No recognized AI/ML expertise or demonstrated AI-powered products or platforms at scale — AI work is limited to ~1 year of agent tooling
Career level is Senior/Staff individual contributor; no evidence of director-level scope, strategic advisory to customers, or organization-wide engineering leadership
No Master's or PhD in an AI-related field, and cloud experience (AWS CDK, Kubernetes) is applied rather than strategic or platform-defining

key_factors.factor: AI expertise and AI product track record

response.justification: The candidate meets the minimum bar: a BS in Computer Science and roughly 19 years of engineering experience spanning backend, frontend, embedded, and performance-critical systems, plus genuine entrepreneurial experience founding GISCollective. However, the role's core competencies are recognized AI expertise, AI-powered product delivery, and director-level strategic influence. Evidence for these is thin: the only AI-relevant work is recent MCP/agentic tooling at re:cinq, there is no advanced AI degree, no shipped AI product or platform cited, and all roles are Senior/Staff IC positions on delivery teams rather than strategy, advisory, or cross-organizational leadership. Community teaching (workshops, conference talks) shows communication ability but not the industry-level thought leadership the role demands. Strong senior engineer, but a poor fit for a Principal AI-strategy position.
